title: Clinical Negligence Expert Witness | Psychology Direct
description: Expert psychological and psychiatric opinion for clinical negligence claims. Breach of duty, causation, and prognosis reports.

blocks.headline: Prove Clinical Negligence Claims with Expert Evidence
blocks.subtitle: We provide detailed breach of duty and causation assessments from experienced psychologists and psychiatrists. Your reports are court-ready, available at Legal Aid rates, and typically completed within 4–6 weeks.

children.text: Our psychologists assess psychological harm arising from medical negligence. They investigate misdiagnosis, delayed treatment, surgical error, and other medical failings. They evaluate whether the breach of duty caused the claimant's psychological condition.
Services include:
Psychological harm assessment (trauma PTSD anxiety depression)
Breach of duty and causation opinions
Prognosis and treatment recommendations
Impact on function and quality of life
Comparative fault analysis
Negligent infliction of emotional distress assessment
Delayed diagnosis psychological impact
Surgical/procedural error impact assessment

Our psychiatrists provide expert medical opinion on mental health conditions stemming from clinical negligence. They assess diagnosis, treatment, medical causation, and prognosis. They're essential when the breach involves psychiatric treatment, medication error, or mental health malpractice.
Services include:
Psychiatric harm assessment and diagnosis
Breach of duty and causation opinions (medical standard of care)
Treatment recommendations and prognosis
Medication error and iatrogenic harm assessment
Delayed psychiatric diagnosis impact
Medical negligence in mental health treatment
Capacity and decision-making post-negligence
Impact on ongoing treatment needs

Your case hinges on proving breach of duty and demonstrating causation. Our experts provide evidence-based opinions on the standard of care and whether it was breached. They also assess whether that breach caused the claimant's harm. Your dedicated Client Manager will advise which expert type best serves your specific breach and causation questions.
Not sure which discipline you need? Try our tool: Psychologist or Psychiatrist? → /tools/psychologist-or-psychiatrist/

cards.title: Mental Health Treatment Negligence
cards.description: Negligent psychiatric or psychological treatment, misdiagnosis, inappropriate medication, or failure to diagnose.

cards.title: Delayed Diagnosis Harm
cards.description: Psychological harm from delayed diagnosis of mental health conditions.

cards.title: Iatrogenic (Doctor-Caused) Injury
cards.description: Psychological or psychiatric harm caused by medical intervention, surgery, or treatment error.

cards.title: Negligent Psychological Intervention
cards.description: Breach of duty in psychological assessment or treatment causing harm.

cards.title: Medical Negligence with Psychological Sequelae
cards.description: Psychological harm from non-psychiatric medical negligence (surgical error, misdiagnosis, etc.).

cards.title: Medication Error & Adverse Effects
cards.description: Psychiatric assessment of harm from medication error, adverse drug reactions, or inappropriate prescribing.

steps.title: Brief Us on the Breach
steps.description: Call us on 01306 879 975 to outline the alleged breach and the claimant's condition. Tell us about the standard of care issue. We'll gather any additional context you provide.

steps.title: Receive Expert CVs Within 24 Hours
steps.description: We'll send 2–3 CVs from psychologists or psychiatrists experienced in breach and causation work. All accept Legal Aid rates.

steps.title: Instruct & We Manage Everything
steps.description: We coordinate all appointments, medical record reviews, and liaison with treating practitioners for you.

steps.title: Detailed Breach & Causation Report
steps.description: Your expert addresses the standard of care, whether it was breached, and whether the breach caused harm. Every report is 100% quality-checked before delivery. Standard turnaround is 4–6 weeks.

caseStudies.title: Psychiatric Treatment Negligence
caseStudies.summary: A patient harmed by negligent psychiatric treatment and medication error required dual assessment. We coordinated psychology and psychiatry experts to opine on breach and causation.

caseStudies.title: Delayed Mental Health Diagnosis
caseStudies.summary: A claimant suffered prolonged psychological harm due to delayed diagnosis of a treatable condition. We matched a psychiatrist to assess breach and causation.

caseStudies.title: Iatrogenic Harm from Medical Negligence
caseStudies.summary: A surgical error led to severe PTSD and psychological dysfunction. We provided a psychologist's detailed assessment of harm and causation.

faqs.question: What is 'breach of duty' in clinical negligence?
faqs.answer: Breach of duty occurs when a healthcare provider fails to meet the standard of care expected of a reasonably competent professional. Examples include failure to diagnose, inappropriate treatment, or negligent delay. Experts opine on whether the defendant fell below this standard.

faqs.question: How do experts assess causation?
faqs.answer: Experts compare what actually happened to what a competent practitioner would have done instead. They assess whether the breach caused the claimant's psychological harm. They also consider alternative causes and the strength of the causal link.

faqs.question: Do I need a psychologist or a psychiatrist?
faqs.answer: For harm from negligent psychiatric treatment or medication error, a psychiatrist is ideal. For psychological harm from other medical negligence, a psychologist works well. Complex cases often benefit from both.

faqs.question: How long does an assessment take?
faqs.answer: Clinical negligence assessments are typically more involved than standard PI assessments. They require detailed medical record review, claimant assessment, and detailed opinion writing. Your expert will confirm exact timelines when you book.

faqs.question: How much does a clinical negligence expert cost?
faqs.answer: Clinical negligence work is typically more complex than standard PI work, so costs may be higher. Our fee calculator gives an instant estimate. For Legal Aid cases, we always work within LAA-set rates.

faqs.question: Will the expert review medical records?
faqs.answer: Yes. Experts will review all relevant medical records, treatment history, diagnostic results, and correspondence. This is essential for breach and causation assessment.

faqs.question: Can an expert opine on damages?
faqs.answer: Experts opine on causation, severity, prognosis, and treatment needs — all relevant to damages. Quantum remains a matter for the court, but psychological/psychiatric evidence strongly informs damages awards.

blocks.heading: Ready to Instruct Your Expert?
blocks.bodyText: We'll match you with an expert who understands your breach and causation challenge. We handle everything from initial matching through to report delivery.
